author: palladinojam
text: @jtrevorchapman Yeah, my first question was going to be how you made out with the Delta issue yesterday. ... I feel like I have a lot of my parameters set up fairly close to where you were with a lot of your original thesis. .i was getting in still no issues on my end consistently

author: jtrevorchapman
text: @palladinojam I was getting in to fewer markets — last week I ran a bunch of recorded data through a backtest to find safe zones. So time based into five 3 minute windows. Delta between ptb and binance/cl and, the delta between bin and cl. Found reliable metrics starting at 6m remaining based

author: jtrevorchapman
text: @Pxlern On the most recent script, 2.29 as of right now. But I pushed out a major defensive upgrade today and will need a few days of data to give an accurate pf post update.

author: MissJea57445728
text: @jtrevorchapman In a typical 15-minute prediction market session, at what timestamp does the system usually commit to a trade? Does it lean towards the early 'price discovery' phase or the late 'momentum' phase?

author: jtrevorchapman
text:
@MissJea57445728 Each regime (distance from ptb) acts differently. Because the each have different safety thresholds. So, if it’s a 50 to 80 regime, and CVD agrees with the other signals, it may enter in the first 20 seconds. 

If it’s a 0-15$ regime, it typically waits four+ minutes to enter.

author: BryanBrygrant
text: @jtrevorchapman How does the confidence calculation work? Is it based on past signals, or something else?

author: jtrevorchapman
text:
@BryanBrygrant three-layer system: Memory > Signals > Defense.

Layer 1 Session Memory (the prior)...

before ANY signals fire, the system already has an opinion. It scans the last 30+ completed sessions and asks: 'when past sessions LOOKED like this one, which side won?"

It computes a YES win
